stemHtml: Can carburettor icing occur when the outside air temperature (OAT) is between +9°C and +27°C, and if so, under what conditions?

options.a: Yes, because the temperature drop in the carburettor can cause ice even at relatively high OAT if humidity is sufficient.
options.b: Yes, but only if the outside air temperature is below +10°C.
options.c: No, carburettor icing is only possible at temperatures close to or below 0°C.
options.d: No, because carburettor icing cannot occur above +20°C.
